hero.title: 3 Days in Chennai
hero.titleAccent: Silk, Street Food & So Much More.
hero.intro: Three days is honestly enough to fall for Chennai. The kind of city that hits you with noise and colour the moment you land, then slowly reveals its charm. Ancient temples next to buzzing bazaars. A long coastline that turns golden at dusk. Food that will ruin you for everything else.
hero.heroImageAlt: T Nagar shopping district Chennai at night with colourful saree stores

overview.heading: Chennai in 3 Days: A South India Short Break That Actually Works
body: Most people underestimate Chennai. They assume it is just a transit city, a stepping stone to Mahabalipuram or Pondicherry. But spend even two full days in the city and you start to see it differently. The temple gopurams that seem to appear from nowhere on busy roads. The extraordinary food. The sheer weight of fabric, gold and colour inside T Nagar.

This 3-day Chennai tour package from Malaysia is designed for people who want to experience South India without a complicated itinerary. You arrive, you settle in. Day two is yours completely, with a private vehicle and driver guide at your disposal from morning until evening. You go where you want, at your own pace. No rush. No coach group.

It is a Chennai holiday that works whether you have been before or this is your first time. Shoppers come for the Kanchipuram silk and the gold jewellery lanes. Food lovers come for the dosas, the Chettinad gravies and the filter coffee you will not stop thinking about. Culture travellers come for Kapaleeshwarar Temple, Marina Beach and the colonial Fort area. Three days in Chennai can hold all of that.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Keep Coming Back to Chennai

items.title: T Nagar: India's Biggest Shopping District by Revenue
items.body: There is nowhere quite like T Nagar. Multi-storey saree stores, narrow lanes of gold jewellery, Pondy Bazaar street food stalls, and the kind of energy that makes you forget time. It is reportedly the highest-revenue shopping district in all of India. Locals, tourists and wedding shoppers all converge here. Come prepared to browse and leave with more than you planned.
items.imageAlt: Pondy Bazaar T Nagar Chennai colourful shopping district

items.title: Your Own Private Vehicle All Day
items.body: No waiting for a tour bus. No fixed route you have to follow. Your dedicated driver guide knows Chennai and will take you wherever you want to go, from temple visits in the morning to evening street food walks in Mylapore. The vehicle runs 10am to 8pm. The day is genuinely yours.
items.imageAlt: Private driver guide car Chennai street

items.title: Kanchipuram Silk Sarees and South Indian Gold
items.body: Chennai is the best place outside Kanchipuram itself to buy authentic Kanchipuram silk sarees. The city's saree emporiums, particularly in Nungambakkam and Mylapore, carry extraordinary selections of handwoven pieces. Gold jewellery in Chennai is also bought and sold at some of the most competitive rates in India. Many Malaysians make the trip just for this.

items.title: Marina Beach and a City That Belongs to the Sea
items.body: Marina Beach is the second-longest urban beach in the world. At sunset, the whole city seems to come here. Street food carts selling corn, sundal and fresh coconut line the promenade. It is not a swimming beach, but as a place to absorb Chennai's soul, nothing beats it. A morning or evening visit is worth building your day around.

days.title: Arrive Chennai
days.description: Your Dhesu representative will be waiting at Chennai International Airport the moment you clear arrivals. Transfer straight to your hotel and check in. The rest of the day is free. Chennai at night near your hotel is worth a short wander. The filter coffee at any local restaurant will set the tone for the trip.
suggestedActivities: Settle in and explore the neighbourhood around your hotel
Try a South Indian thali dinner at a nearby restaurant
Filter coffee at a local cafe

days.title: Full Day Chennai with Private Driver Guide
days.description: After breakfast, your private vehicle and driver guide are available from 10am to 8pm. This is your day to design. Most visitors use it for a mix of sightseeing and shopping. Your driver guide will suggest a sensible route and give you context at every stop, but you set the agenda.
suggestedActivities: Kapaleeshwarar Temple in Mylapore (ancient Dravidian architecture, morning is quieter)
Marina Beach promenade walk or sunrise visit
T Nagar for sarees, jewellery and street shopping
Pondy Bazaar street food walk (murukku, sundal, pav bhaji)
San Thome Cathedral (a surprising piece of history near the beach)
Ampa Skywalk for modern shopping and PVR cinema

days.title: Free Morning, Depart Chennai
days.description: Checkout is at 12pm (noon) unless we arrange otherwise. Use the morning at your own pace. A final round of shopping in T Nagar, a slow breakfast with more filter coffee, or just a walk to Marina Beach one last time. Your driver will transfer you to Chennai International Airport for your onward flight.
suggestedActivities: Final filter coffee and masala dosa at a local restaurant
Marina Beach morning walk before checkout

sections.heading: Chennai Tour Package from Malaysia: What You Actually Get
sections.body:
A lot of Chennai tour packages list attractions but leave out the thing that matters most: how free you actually are on the trip. This 3-day package gives you one full day with a private vehicle and driver guide who knows the city. The rest is genuinely your time. No packed coach. No group itinerary to stick to.

That one day of private access makes a real difference in a city like Chennai, where the good stuff is spread out. Getting between T Nagar, Mylapore and Marina Beach on your own takes patience. With a dedicated driver, you cover more ground and spend your energy actually experiencing things rather than figuring out transport.

For Malaysian travellers, Chennai is about a 3.5 to 4 hour flight from Kuala Lumpur. Direct flights operate daily from KLIA. The time zone difference is minimal (2.5 hours behind Malaysia), which means almost no jet lag. It is genuinely one of the easier India trips to take.

sections.heading: What to Shop for in Chennai on a Short Trip
sections.body:
Chennai is one of the best cities in South India for shopping, and T Nagar is the reason. The neighbourhood is packed with saree stores, jewellery shops and bazaars. Here is what most travellers come looking for:

Kanchipuram silk sarees are the top buy. Look for stores in Nungambakkam and the Ranganathan Street area in T Nagar. Prices range from RM200 to several thousand depending on the weight of silk and complexity of the zari work. Your driver guide can recommend reputable shops.

Gold and diamond jewellery at competitive rates. Chennai has some of the best gold prices in India. Families travelling together often combine a temple visit with jewellery shopping in the same trip.

Cotton fabrics, block prints and traditional crafts are widely available in Pondy Bazaar and the smaller lanes off Usman Road.

For food, take back packets of murukku, ribbon pakoda, filter coffee powder and home-made pickles from the local grocery stores near any temple area.

sections.heading: South India Tour from Malaysia: Is 3 Days in Chennai Enough?
sections.body:
Three days in Chennai is a good fit for specific types of trips. It works best if you are: adding Chennai as an extension to another India destination, making a dedicated shopping trip, or testing the waters with South India for the first time before committing to a longer itinerary.

If you want a fuller picture of Tamil Nadu, consider the 4-day South India temples package that includes Tirupati, Thiruvannamalai and Kanchipuram. Or if you want to combine beach, heritage and backwaters, the 5-day Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty package gives you the hill station experience on top of city culture.

But for a short, self-directed South India holiday from Malaysia? Three days in Chennai, done properly with a private vehicle and a smart hotel choice, is genuinely satisfying. People who come for the shopping usually wish they had packed an extra bag.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit India from Malaysia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian citizens can apply for an India e-Visa online before travel. The standard tourist e-Visa is valid for 30 days from date of entry and covers tourism, recreation and short visits. The application is done online at the official India e-Visa portal. Dhesu provides guidance on the process when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airports does this Chennai package fly from?
items.answer: This is a land-only package, meaning hotel, driver guide and transfers are included but flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly direct from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chennai International Airport (MAA). Airlines including AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines and IndiGo operate on this route. We can assist with flight recommendations on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Chennai from Malaysia?
items.answer: November to February is the most comfortable time to visit Chennai. Temperatures sit between 20 and 30 degrees Celsius, mornings are cool and the evenings are pleasant. March to May gets hot, sometimes hitting 40 degrees. June to September is the southwest monsoon season, which is less severe in Chennai than the rest of Tamil Nadu. The northeast monsoon (October to December) brings heavier rain to Chennai specifically, so plan accordingly if travelling in November.

items.question: Is Chennai safe for Malaysian tourists?
items.answer: Chennai is widely regarded as one of the safer major Indian cities for tourists. The city has a large Tamil diaspora community with ties to Malaysia, so many locals are familiar with Malaysian visitors and some even speak a little Bahasa Malaysia or Tamil. Standard travel precautions apply: keep valuables secure, use your hotel safe, and let the driver guide handle any situations that feel unfamiliar.

items.question: Can I extend this package to include Mahabalipuram or Pondicherry?
items.answer: Absolutely. Mahabalipuram (about 60km from Chennai) and Pondicherry (about 150km) are popular day trips or overnight extensions from Chennai. Talk to us before booking and we can build in an extra day or two covering the UNESCO-listed shore temples at Mahabalipuram, the French Quarter of Pondicherry and the stretch of coastal road between them. Many Malaysians combine all three into a 5-day South India itinerary.

items.question: What is included in the private driver guide service?
items.answer: Your driver guide is available from 10am to 8pm on Day 2 of the package. The vehicle is air-conditioned (Toyota Etios for 2 passengers, Toyota Innova for 4, Tempo Traveller for groups of 8 or more). The guide can give you context on temples and historical sites, recommend restaurants and shops, handle parking and local navigation, and generally make the day run smoothly. They are not a formal licensed tour guide for historical commentary but they know the city well.

items.question: Is this package suitable for families with children?
items.answer: Yes. The private vehicle format makes this one of the better India packages for families with young children, because you are never tied to a group schedule. The hotel breakfast, flexible day structure and 24-hour checkout policy all help when travelling with kids. Chennai has wide roads, malls with clean facilities and plenty of air-conditioned spaces. The beach at Marina is a hit with children.

items.question: Can I get halal food in Chennai?
items.answer: Yes, Chennai has a large Muslim community and halal food is widely available. Biryani restaurants, halal-certified restaurants near mosque areas, and street food stalls serving Muslim-friendly options are all over the city. Your driver guide can point you to well-known halal restaurants in Triplicane, Thousand Lights and the Royapettah area.
